articles.title: Zimbabwe's iconic stone birds were taken by colonialists. Finally, they're all back home

articles.title: Mugabe's son pleads guilty to pointing a gun in South Africa
articles.description: Bellarmine Mugabe was arrested in February following the shooting of a 23-year-old man at his home in Johannesburg.

articles.title: Zimbabwe play challenges proposed constitutional changes
articles.description: Bodo is a one-man performance that draws on Ngano, a traditional Shona storytelling method, blending it with modern visual and audio elements to create an immersive experience. Through this fusion of tradition and contemporary theatre, Guzha seeks not only to entertain but to provoke reflection

more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: National Foods Expands Manufacturing Base as Demand Drives Capacity Growth
more.description: HARARE – National Foods Holdings Limited is accelerating its industrial expansion strategy following a strong demand outlook, with three new production plants commissioned in 2025 already reshaping the country’s food supply dynamics. The country’s largest food producer established dedicated lines for pasta, breakfast cereals, and biscuits, marking one of the most significant capital investment programmes […]The post National Foods Expands Manufacturing Base as Demand Drives Capacity Growth appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: Zimbabwe Inflation Edges Higher as Transport and Food Costs Intensify Pressure
more.description: HARARE – Zimbabwe recorded a renewed uptick in inflation in April, driven largely by rising transport and food prices, signalling mounting cost-of-living pressures for households and increasing input costs for businesses. Latest data from the Zimbabwe National Statistics Agency (Zimstat) shows that the Zimbabwe Gold (ZiG) month-on-month inflation rate rose to 1.1% in April, up […]The post Zimbabwe Inflation Edges Higher as Transport and Food Costs Intensify Pressure appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: Afreximbank targets Southern African fuel corridors with Zimbabwe pipeline plans
more.description: HARARE – Zimbabwe is set to feature prominently in a new US$3bn energy financing push by African Export-Import Bank, as the continental lender moves to reconfigure Africa’s fuel supply chains away from traditional Middle Eastern routes and towards intra-African trade. more.title: Zimbabwe Showcases Tourism Potential at ZITF 2026 Amid Governance Concerns
more.description: BULAWAYO – Zimbabwe’s flagship trade and tourism showcase, the Zimbabwe International Trade Fair 2026, has once again highlighted the country’s ambition to position itself as a competitive global destination. However, the event has unfolded against a backdrop of growing debate over governance and the country’s international image. more.title: At Beijing auto show, Chinese carmakers flaunt new technologies as global competition heats up
more.description: BEIJING — China’s top automakers are showcasing their latest models and technologies from intelligent driving to ultrafast charging in Beijing as they compete with global rivals in overseas markets.
